data.title: Earnings Snapshot: MongoDB sets FY expectations above estimates, tops FQ3 estimates
Vecima announces cost restructuring
data.description:
• Vecima Networks (TSX:VCM:CA) announced it has undertaken a workforce reorganization of approximately 12% to align teams and program investments with customers' needs while also enhancing operating efficiency.
  • The resulting workforce reduction is expected to deliver annualized savings of approximately $17.5M, starting in the second half of fiscal 2025.
  • Vecima expects to record related reorganization costs of approximately $3M in the second quarter of fiscal 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Loblaw Companies to issue $400M of senior unsecured notes
data.description:
• Loblaw Companies (TSX:L:CA) said on Monday that it has agreed to issue, on a private placement basis, to qualified accredited investors in the provinces of Canada, $400M aggregate principal amount of senior unsecured notes, at 3.564% per annum, that will mature on December 12, 2029.
  • The notes will be sold at par and are being offered on an agency basis by a syndicate of agents led by RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, TD Securities, CIBC Capital Markets and Scotia Capital.
  • The net proceeds of the offering will be used by the company to redeem all of its issued and outstanding second preferred shares, Series B, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Toll Brothers fiscal Q4 earnings climb past consensus on strong demand; issues 2025 outlook
data.description:
Toll Brothers (NYSE:TOL) stock dipped 2.2% in Monday after-hours trading even after the homebuilder turned in fiscal Q4 earnings that rose past the average analyst estimate amid still-strong housing demand, and also issued guidance for fiscal-year 2025.

The company expects 2025 home deliveries of 11.2K-11.6K, aligning with the 11.4K Visible Alpha consensus. Adjusted home sales gross margin is expected to be 27.25%.

For Q1 2025, deliveries are targeted to be 1.90K-2.10K, also in-line with the $953.1M Visible Alpha estimate. Toll (NYSE:TOL) expects adjusted home sales gross margin of 26.25%.

Adjusted EPS of $4.63 for the fourth quarter ended Oct. 31, 2024, topping the $4.34 Wall Street consensus, climbed from $3.60 in the previous quarter and $4.11 a year earlier.

Q4 revenue of $3.33B, vs. $3.17B expected, advanced from $2.73 in Q3 and $3.02M in the year-ago period.

Adjusted home sales gross margin, which excludes interest and inventory write-downs, retreated to 27.9% from 28.8% in Q3 and 29.1% in Q4 2023.

Backlog value fell 7% from a year ago to $6.47B. Homes in backlog slid 9% Y/Y to 5,996.

During the three-month period, Toll (TOL) bought back some 1.3M shares at an average price of $150.19 per share for a total of $200.9M.

Conference call on Dec. 10, at 8:30 a.m. ET.

data.title: US Rep. Jared Moskowitz discloses buys of several Mag 7 stocks
data.description:
• U.S. House of Representative member Jared Moskowitz (D-Fla.) disclosed a swath of recent stock purchases, including several from the Magnificent 7, such as Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), Meta (NASDAQ:META), Google (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA).
  • According to the filing, a Periodic Transaction Report, the 43-year-old congressman, who was elected to the House last year, made three purchases of Amazon stock ranging from $1,000 to $15,000 on November 8.
  • He invested in several other tech names as well, including Analog Devices (ADI), Broadcom (AVGO), Accenture (ACN), Oracle (ORCL), Qualcomm (QCOM) and Texas Instruments (TXN). These also all ranged from $1,000 to $15,000.
  • Outside of tech, other stock purchases included Chevron (CVX), Exxon Mobil (XOM), Lockheed Martin (LMT) and McDonald's (MCD). The disclosure details the congressman purchased stocks from 30 corporations in total.
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Vail Resorts beats expectations, raises guidance
data.description:
Vail Resorts (NYSE:MTN) reported a slightly wider loss in the fiscal first quarter even as sales were marginally higher as costs associated with its two-year resource efficiency transformation plan offset an 8% price increase to its season passes.

For the quarter ended October 31, the company lost $4.61 per share, a penny lower from last year, but 44 cents better than Wall Street expected. Sales were up 0.7% to $260.3M, $10.5M above expectations.

“Our first fiscal quarter historically operates at a loss, given that our North American and European mountain resorts are generally not open for ski season. The quarter's results were driven by winter operations in Australia and summer activities in North America,” CEO Kirsten Lynch said, adding that favorable conditions have allowed some resorts to be opened earlier than anticipated, including the back bowls at Vail Mountain, opening the earliest in the season since 2018.

By segment, mountain net revenue increased 0.5% to $800K driven by an increase in summer visitation to North American resorts, while mountain EBITDA loss of $144.1M widened 3.3% from last year.

Lodging segment revenue increased 6.9% on positive weather conditions in the Grand Teton region, and higher dining and golf revenue. Lodging EBITDA increased to $4.3M from a loss of $236K last year.

Resort net revenue, which combines the mountain and lodging segments, increased 2.3% to $260.2M, while EBITDA loss for the segment narrowed slightly to a loss of $139.7M.

Looking ahead to the fiscal year ending July 31, 2025, Vail Resorts (MTN) raised its net income guidance to $240M to $316M from prior guidance of $224M to $300M, largely due to the sale of real estate. The current guidance assumes a continuation of the current economic environment, normal weather conditions for the 2024/2025 North American and European ski season and the 2025 Australian ski season, and foreign exchange rates.

Shares are up 3% in after-hours trading.

data.title: The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son announces new $100M share repurchase program
data.description:
• The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son Limited (NYSE:NTB) board has approved a $100M ordinary share repurchase program.
  • The Bank is authorized to purchase up to 2.7M of its ordinary shares through December 31, 2025.
  • The new share repurchase program will come into effect on January 1, 2025 with the termination of the current 2.1M share repurchase program that was announced on July 22, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oracle in charts: Revenue from Americas rises most Y/Y among geographies in FQ2
Crane Company announces promotion of Alejandro Alcala to COO
data.description:
• Crane Company (NYSE:CR) has promoted Alejandro Alcala to chief operating officer, effective immediately. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dover announces CFO retirement; names successor
data.description:
• Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) announced that Brad Cerepak, senior vice president and chief financial officer plans to retire on January 31, 2025.
  • Christopher Woenker, CFO of the company's engineered products and climate & sustainability technologies segments, will succeed Cerepak as senior vice president and CFO. 
  • DOV shares were up over 5% in after hours trading on Monday. 
  • Source: Press Release
